back_translation: When He saw the crowds, He went up on the mountain, and when He had sat down, His disciples came to Him.
Then He opened His mouth and taught them, saying:
'Those who are poor in heart/spirit are blessed, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.'
'Those who mourn are blessed, for they will receive comforting.'
'The humble-in-heart are blessed, for they will receive the earth as an inheritance.'
'Those who hunger and thirst for righteousness are blessed, for they will be filled to satisfaction.'
'Those with a merciful heart are blessed, for they will receive divine mercy.'
'Those who are pure in heart are blessed, for they will see God.'
'Peacemakers are blessed, for they will be called sons of God.'
'Those who are persecuted because of righteousness are blessed, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.'
'You are blessed when they insult you, persecute you, and accuse you falsely with every kind of evil because of me.'
'Rejoice and be glad, for your reward in heaven is great, for they persecuted the prophets who came before you in the same way.'
'You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, by what will it be made salty again? It is no longer useful for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled by people.'
'You are the light of the world. A city set on a mountain cannot be hidden.'
'No one lights a lamp and puts a bucket over it; instead, they set it on a lampstand, so that it gives light to everyone in the house.'
'In the same way, let your light shine before people, so that they may see your good deeds and give glory to your Father who dwells in heaven.'
'Do not think that I came to overthrow the Law or the words of the prophets. I did not come to overthrow, but to fulfill.'
'I tell you truly: as long as heaven and earth have not passed away, not one iota letter or one stroke will pass away from the Law, until everything is accomplished.'

'Therefore, whoever violates even one of the least of these commandments, and teaches others to do so, that person will be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever practices and teaches them, that person will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.'
'For I tell you: if your righteousness does not surpass the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.'
'You have heard the words spoken to the ancients: "Do not kill a person," and "Whoever kills a person will be judged."'

'But I tell you: whoever is angry with his brother [without cause] will be judged. And whoever says to his brother "Raca" will be judged by the Sanhedrin council. And whoever says "You fool" will be sent into the fire of hell.'
'Therefore, if you bring your offering to the altar, and there remember that your brother has a grievance against you,'
'leave your offering there before the altar and go. Reconcile with your brother first, then come back and offer your offering.'
'Quickly settle with your adversary while you are still on the way with him, lest the adversary hand you over to the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and you be thrown into prison.'
'I tell you truly: you will not get out of there until you have paid the smallest last coin.'
'You have heard the words spoken: "Do not commit adultery."'
'But I tell you: whoever looks at another woman with lustful desire has already committed adultery with her in his heart.'
'If your right eye causes you to stumble-astray, gouge it out and throw it away; for it is better that one member of your body perish than that your whole body be thrown into hell.'
'And if your right hand causes you to stumble-astray, cut it off and throw it away; for it is better that one member of your body perish than that your whole body go down into hell.'
'It has also been said: "Whoever divorces his wife, let that man give her a certificate of divorce."'

'But I tell you: whoever divorces his wife, except for the cause of sexual immorality, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a woman who has been divorced, that person also commits adultery.'
'Again, you have heard the words spoken to the ancients: "Do not break your oath, but fulfill your oaths that you have given to the Lord."'
'But I tell you: do not swear at all. Do not invoke heaven, for heaven is the throne of God;'
'nor invoke the earth, for the earth is the footstool of His royal feet; nor invoke Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great royal King.'
'Do not swear by your head at all, for you cannot make even a single hair white or black.'
'But let your word be: "Yes means yes, no means no." Whatever is more than this comes from the evil one.'
'You have heard the words spoken: "An eye in place of an eye, a tooth in place of a tooth."'
'But I tell you: do not resist an evil person. Whoever strikes your right cheek, turn the other cheek to him as well.'
'And whoever wants to sue you and take your inner garment, let him have your outer robe as well.'
'And whoever compels you to walk one mile, go with him two miles.'
'Whoever asks of you, give to him; and whoever wants to borrow from you, do not turn your face away from him.'
'You have heard the words spoken: "Love your neighbor," and "Hate your enemy."'
'But I tell you: love your enemies [bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you], and pray for those who persecute you,'
'so that you may be sons of your Father who dwells in heaven; for He causes His sun to rise and shine on the evil and the good, and He sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.'
'For if you love only those who love you, what reward will you receive? Even tax collectors do the same thing, do they not?'
'And if you greet only your brothers, what are you doing more than others? Even Gentiles do the same thing, do they not?'
'Therefore, be those who are perfectly complete, just as your Father who dwells in heaven is perfectly complete.'
